titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 731

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=970 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:12:46.036855+00 | translation moa=971 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:12:46.026128+00

p.cl: Omnibus Christi fidelibus ad quos presens scriptum peruenerit Clarebaldus de Esseby salutem .

Nouerit vniuersitas uestra me
dimisisse ad perpetuam firmam deo et beato Cuthberto et sancte Ebbe et Monachis dunelmensibus in perpetuum apud
Coldingham deo seruientibus duas piscarias super twedam . scilicet vnam sub gardino de Fissewic et aliam apud schipeswel quas
dominus meus walterus olifard mihi et heredibus meis dedit pro humagio et seruicio meo . concessi ecciam dictis monachis dictas
piscarias in perpetuum tenendas de me et heredibus mis libere et quiete ab omne consuetudine exaccione et demanda preter
octo solidos annuos quos dicti monachi michi et heredibus meis singulis annis pro omnibus persoluent ad duos terminos . silicet quatuor
solidos ad festum sancti Iohannis baptiste et quatuor solidos ad festum sancti Martini in hieme . Ego autem et heredes mei dictas piscarias
dictis monachis contra omnes homines warantizabimus et quietos faciemus dictos monachos ab omni forinseco seruicio tam uersus
dominum Regem quam uersus dominum meum et heredes suos .
et in huius rei testimonium sigillum meum huic scripto apposui .

Hiis testibus .
Domino Willelm de Morthington’ . Domino Alano de Swintona . Waltero . Andrea et Alexandro de Paxtona . Ada de Prendergest . Iohanne
filio Helye . Mauricio de Aytona . Ada et Bertramo de Ristona . Radulfo preposito . Radulfo seruiente . et multis
aliis
To all Christ's faithful to whom the present writing shall have come, Clarebald of Ashby sends greeting.

Know you all that I have let at perpetual ferme, to God, blessed Cuthbert, Saint Ebbe, and the monks of Durham in perpetuity serving God at Coldingham, two fisheries on the Tweed, that is, one below the garden at Fishwick and the other at Shipswell, which my lord Walter Oliphant gave to me and my heirs in return for my homage and service; I have also granted to the said monks the said fisheries to be held of me and my heirs in perpetuity, free and quit from every custom, exaction, and demand, except for eight shillings which the said monks shall pay me and my heirs at two terms each year in return for everything, that is, two shillings at the feast of Saint John the Baptist and four shillings at the feast of Saint Martin in the winter. I and my heirs shall warrant the said fisheries to the said monks against all men and make the said monks quit of every forinsec service both towards the lord king and towards my lord and his heirs.
In testimonty of this matter I have applied my seal to this writing.
